name: ultracode
description: Fan out Codex subprocess workers for deep code investigation, planning, review, and edits — through one command that takes a task fan-out, a barrier-free steps[] DAG, an imperative Workflow script, or a saved workflow. Adds adversarial verification, budgets, journaled resume, a live run dashboard, pluggable transports (exec / app-server), warm-context resume, and transient-error retry.

interface.longDescription: Ultracode gives Codex a subprocess-backed workflow engine for parallel investigation, planning, verification, and edits. Workers run read-only by default, or writable in an isolated git worktree whose diff is collected back. There is one command: hand it a task sentence (fixed-role fan-out), a steps[] DAG (barrier-free), a workers_spec[] panel, an imperative Workflow script (with fanout()/dag() helpers), or a saved @workflow. A live React run dashboard streams every worker; it adds budgets, journaled resume, adversarial and perspective-diverse verification, warm-context resume, transient-error retry with backoff, and a pluggable transport (exec or app-server). Worker output returns to the parent thread, which synthesizes the evidence and integrates the result.
defaultPrompt: Use Ultracode to investigate this change
Run parallel Codex workers on this bug
